anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Observer-independent clocks and nonhuman timing systems, such as cesium clocks, pulsars, GPS satellites, automated trading systems, and biological clocks.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence sharply for the physics claim and keeps the idea as a renamed or applied extension, not a candidate discovery.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: They count transitions, coordinate records, and guide action without a conscious holder of past, present, and future. This does not refute the phenomenology of legibility for persons, but it does refute any strong claim that physical clocks require human temporal holding in order to measure.

claim_units: Clock time is operational counting, not the full meaning of time.
A clock reading becomes legible only within a held structure of remembered past, attended present, and intended future.
Physics can coordinate events and relations but cannot replace lived temporal participation.
The felt present is neither mere illusion nor the only real thing, it is where memory, care, and intention become actionable.
The modern use case is anxiety, rumination, future dread, burnout, and achievement-contingent self-worth.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Music, sport, and high-skill performance coaching
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Coaching cues that explicitly bind learned memory, present sensation, and near-future intention will reduce choking and post-error rumination better than now-only cues for anxious high achievers.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: A performer does not succeed by collapsing into the instant. Effective presence carries prior repetitions, current sensory feedback, and intended phrase or play without becoming self-judgment.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Performance and distress are fully explained by practice hours, sleep, coach quality, and generic arousal control, with no added value from temporal-width cues.

exact_claim: Clocks count coordinated transitions but do not contain the lived holding of memory, attention, and expectation. The healthy present is a disciplined width that carries past and future without collapsing into rumination, thin-now mindfulness, or future dread.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: In a two-week pilot with stable adults under deadline pressure, the practice reduces both rumination and future anxiety without increasing planning compulsion, dissociation, or self-surveillance.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: A held-width practice adds value beyond present-only mindfulness and ordinary time-perspective psychoeducation.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Randomized pilot with rumination, future anxiety, distress, missed-obligation, and adverse-effect measures.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: No advantage over balanced time perspective coaching, ACT-style values planning, or present-only mindfulness, or any increase in anxiety, dissociation, scrupulosity, or avoidance.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Students taught Einstein-style clock coordination plus Bergson-Augustine duration will be less likely to conclude that relativity proves the felt present is simply unreal, while retaining correct physics answers.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The clock-phenomenology distinction has pedagogical value.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Pre-post physics teaching experiment with misconception coding and accuracy checks.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Students make the same illusion claim at the same rate, or physics accuracy drops because phenomenology confuses the operational account.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Examen, recollection, narrative confession, and review practices will show different warnings and repairs under stress than pure-present or no-thought practices, even after controlling for teacher style and clinical risk.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Temporal-width practices form a real family across traditions and not just analyst vocabulary.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-source manual coding plus practitioner interviews across Christian, Buddhist, Jewish, Sufi, and secular practices.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings and repairs are better predicted by institution, teacher style, intensity, and participant mental health than by temporal-width coding.

literature_search_queries: Augustine Confessions XI time distention mind measures time
Bergson clock counts simultaneities conscious spectator duration
Husserl retention primal impression protention internal time consciousness
Heidegger ecstatic temporality future having been present
Zimbardo Boyd balanced time perspective anxiety depression rumination
Rovelli partial observables time relational clocks
Dogen Uji being-time past present future no holder
meditation adverse effects anxiety dissociation mindfulness present moment

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds relativity, Dogen, and a modern anxiety practice frame.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Major downgrade. The holding triad is inherited almost directly.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close. Augustine already frames time as held in the soul through memory, attention, and expectation, and treats measurement as occurring in the mind.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate calls this externalized holding and links it to operational physics and burnout.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Strongest near-neighbor. Pushes the core claim toward renamed rather than discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Extremely close. Bergson says clock measurement counts simultaneities, while lived duration depends on consciousness organizing past and present.
near_neighbors.source: Henri Bergson, Time and Free Will, Project Gutenberg, https://www.gutenberg.org/cache/epub/56852/pg56852-images.html

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate turns this into a practical doctrine for time anxiety and puts clocks under the same pressure.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades phenomenological novelty. The remaining contribution would have to be applied and testable.
near_neighbors.overlap: Husserl's retention, primal impression, and protention already describe a unified temporal field with past and anticipated future in the living present.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate keeps a more Augustine and Bergson style holder, while Heidegger is less interested in clock legibility as such.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Further downgrade. The existential structure of carried past and projected future is not new.
near_neighbors.overlap: Heidegger's ecstatic temporality already binds future, having-been, and present as the structure of care and intelligibility.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate uses spiritual-philosophical language rather than psychometric time-perspective categories.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades the human-condition and practice claim. The candidate must outperform balanced time perspective, not merely rename it.
near_neighbors.overlap: Balanced time perspective already treats past, present, and future orientations as behaviorally and clinically relevant, with links to depression, anxiety, self-regulation, and rumination.
near_neighbors.source: Zimbardo and Boyd, Putting Time in Perspective, https://scienceofbehaviorchange.org/wp-content/uploads/2017/10/TimePerspective.Zimbardo.1999.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: They do not require human holding for physical time relations.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Cuts against the physics-side claim that clocks are parasitic on a human temporal holder.
near_neighbors.overlap: Relational and correlation-based accounts already remove independent flowing time and treat clock readings as relations among systems.
near_neighbors.source: Rovelli, Partial Observables, https://arxiv.org/abs/gr-qc/0110035, and Page and Wootters, Evolution Without Evolution, https://cds.cern.ch/record/143641

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate admits this pressure but still recommends held width as a healthy present.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Functions as an anomaly. It weakens any metaphysical reading and permits only a pragmatic practice claim.
near_neighbors.overlap: Dogen's being-time refuses a holder outside time and treats each being as time rather than as a subject holding time.

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a discovery. Treat as a renamed synthesis of Augustine, Bergson, Husserl, Heidegger, and balanced time perspective.
Rebuild as a bounded practice protocol called temporal-width training, not as a claim about what physics cannot measure.
Compare directly against balanced time perspective, ACT values work, time management, Examen, and present-only mindfulness.
Close-read Dogen Uji and Nagarjuna MMK 19 as anomaly pressure against reifying past, present, and future.
Add explicit safety stops for trauma memory, dissociation, acute anxiety, severe depression, mania, addiction withdrawal, and compulsive self-management.
Run practitioner interviews before any teaching promotion. Ask for failure cases, not agreement.
novelty_adjustment: Lower novelty from 0.55 to 0.26. Human-condition fit is direct but narrow: stable adults with deadline stress, rumination, future dread, and achievement-contingent self-worth. It is not for acute panic, trauma activation, severe depression, mania, psychosis, addiction withdrawal, dissociation, or people avoiding concrete obligations. Publishability should stay low unless it is framed as a tested temporal-integration practice rather than a metaphysical discovery.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners identify it as ordinary Augustine, Bergson, Husserl, Heidegger, balanced time perspective, ACT, Examen, or mindfulness safety guidance, and say it does not alter instruction or repair.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the held-width distinction to predict who benefits, who destabilizes, and what repair is needed better than existing present-moment or time-perspective language.
questions: Does the held-width instruction add anything you do not already get from Augustine, Bergson, Husserl, or balanced time perspective?
Have you seen present-only instructions make anxious practitioners thinner, more avoidant, or more dissociated?
Would this model change how you guide a deadline-stressed practitioner, or is it just familiar language?
What concrete failure case would make you stop using this framing?
relevant_practitioners: Philosophers or physicists who teach relativity and time
Soto Zen teachers familiar with Uji
Mindfulness teachers and meditation safety clinicians
Therapists or coaches using balanced time perspective, ACT, or burnout interventions
Practitioners who use Examen, recollection, or daily review

primary_pair: Bergson, Time and Free Will, chapter 2, Project Gutenberg, https://www.gutenberg.org/cache/epub/56852/pg56852-images.html
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model risks smuggling phenomenological legibility into physical measurement. A cesium clock, pulsar, or automated control system can count transitions without a humanly held past and future. The practice claim also risks turning anxious people into managers of their own temporal width.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The audit used accessible primary texts, SEP articles, source cards, and open academic summaries. It did not read Husserl in German, Heidegger in German, Bergson in French, the full Dogen Uji translation history, or full clinical literature on Time Perspective Therapy. No practitioner interviews were run.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Augustine already gives the core triad: past and future are present in the soul as memory and expectation, and time is measured in the mind. Bergson is an even closer clock-side source: the clock does not measure lived duration, it counts simultaneities while consciousness preserves succession. Together they contain most of the candidate's structure before the physics and practice framing are added.

title: Time Is Held, Not Counted
unlike_statement: Unlike Augustine, Bergson, Husserl, Heidegger, balanced time perspective, and relational time physics, this idea's possible distinct contribution is a narrow practice hypothesis: explicitly train a present that carries memory and intention, then test whether it reduces both rumination and future dread better than present-only mindfulness, balanced time perspective coaching, or ordinary planning.
